steps.title: Identify the defining contrast
steps.content: Robert opens with “For you it's different,” immediately signaling that he and Andrew have fundamentally different natures. Everything that follows—describing Andrew as “wedded to the soil” and grouping him with their father—develops this contrast.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice D correctly identifies that the passage emphasizes a difference between Andrew and Robert—Robert explicitly contrasts Andrew's bond to the soil and farm with an implicit difference in Robert himself. The other choices don't fit: they claim shared goals, mystery-solving, or vacation description, none of which appear here. That matches choice D — D is correct.
